authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 438.208 - Coordination and continuity of care
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This federal section governs continuity and coordination requirements in managed care, directly shaping what must be in place before transitions, referrals, and care continuity routing can proceed.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: dependency intake and transition routing checks

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 441.725 - Person-centered service plan
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This source defines service-plan and coordination artifacts required for elderly and disability HCBS services, making it the control baseline for evidence and handoff completeness.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Home and Community Based Services Fact Sheet
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS person-centered planning and HCBS guidance explains the operational context that makes the lane materially different from generic coordination work.

authoritative_sources.title: HCBS Settings Rule resources
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official settings guidance frames participant direction, service delivery context, and coordination implications for transitions in HCBS and disability services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service plan transition and participant-driven handoff review

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule (45 CFR Part 164 Subpart E)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Privacy and disclosure constraints determine safe record-sharing across teams and therefore what this specialist can transmit during handoff routing.

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate required person-centered, consent, and policy context before ownership transfer.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team coordination and routing
workflow_stages.description: Coordinate which adjacent teams own planning, operations, clinical escalation, rights, and compliance actions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Blocker escalation and evidence correction
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when owner ambiguity, missing evidence, or conflicting source rules prevents safe progression.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and handoff completion
workflow_stages.description: Define explicit closure criteria and handoff status where owner transfer and blocker state are explicitly confirmed.

artifacts: Person-centered service plans and plan updates
Care team consent and information-sharing records
Owner routing notices and closure confirmations

allowed: Validate coordination prerequisites and maintain evidence-aware handoff routing.
Track dependency gaps, ownership conflicts, and blocker states across team boundaries.
Issue explicit routing, escalation, and closure confirmations.
Escalate requests that require clinical, legal, financial, or policy adjudication to adjacent owners.
escalate: Missing consent, evidence artifacts, or prerequisite policy context.
Conflicting tenant policy vs public guidance on routing ownership.
Safety or rights-sensitive allegations that imply specialist escalation.
refuse: Direct clinical treatment, nursing intervention, or licensing decisions.
Financial approvals, payer adjudication, reimbursement resolution, or legal representation.
Execution authority such as direct dispatching, staffing mandates, or service delivery completion decisions.

domain_failure_modes: Handoff attempts without completed person-centered planning or consent context cause unsafe continuity gaps.
Ambiguous ownership across nursing, OT, and social support creates deadlock or duplicate effort if not explicitly tracked.
Privacy or tenant policy conflicts can block lawful data sharing and invalidate routing decisions.
Overly broad coordination requests can pull the lane into adjacent clinical/legal/financial authority.
Stale or contradictory sources can make route ownership decisions incorrect unless escalated explicitly.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

notes: The lane is constrained as a coordination authority by design: it moves handoffs, enforces artifact readiness, and identifies blockers without assuming clinical, legal, or financial decision authority.
Any tenant-specific policy conflicts, consent thresholds, or rights procedures must be supplied at runtime via orchestrator retrieval before advancing high-risk routing.
